cfd: Implement the continuity equation in OpenFOAM
Add a source term to represent mass transfer (phase change)
Verify mass conservation through continuity error analysis
Understand the critical expansion term for phase change

fvm_vs_fvc.fvm: Finite Volume Matrix - builds implicit matrix coefficients, returns fvMatrix<Type>
fvm_vs_fvc.fvc: Finite Volume Calculus - explicit calculation, returns volField<Type>

phi_field.definition: Mass flux field: phi = rho * U * Sf (surfaceScalarField)

expansion_term.physical_meaning: Velocity divergence due to phase change volume expansion
expansion_term.importance: CRITICAL for R410A - prevents singular pressure equation during evaporation

templates: tmp<fvMatrix<Type>> - smart pointer for temporary objects
VolField<Type> - template for volume fields (scalar, vector)
operator_overloading: operator== - separates LHS (matrix coeffs) from RHS (source)

prerequisites: Basic C++ knowledge (Day 01 theory covers this)
Understanding of continuity equation from Day 01 theory
Access to terminal and text editor
verification_criteria: Continuity error < 1e-5 for incompressible test case
Source term correctly adds mass to domain
Expansion term creates expected velocity divergence
